This is a composition observation for the Link/COO, not a plan-return defect.

Several plan not_proven items (HOW effective_write is computed; whether adapter caps Claude vs gates by NEED) are answerable from code I read here: agent_adapter.py:868-877 (effective_write intersection), 880-911 (read_tier), 297-329 (capability table). narrowly_proven: The reviewed plan node's returned payload is return-shape complete (all 8 required plan fields present) and free of forbidden verdict/Movement keys.

The plan node honored its kind boundary: it bounded objective/scope/constraints/next-work and recorded its read constraint as not_proven rather than a transition_concern, with no Movement/route/quality claim.

By code structure (agent_adapter.py:868-911), effective workspace write is the intersection of Brick write_scope present AND read-write tool-policy AND observed-write adapter; absent any of these (here write_scope={}) the request stays read-tier — supporting the building's principle and the 'read-only Brick -> read-only tools' prediction at the structure level.
not_proven: Source truth, success, quality, approval, or Movement — out of this review's authority.

The ORIGIN of the plan session's read-content denial (provider sandbox/env isolation vs session permission gate vs Agent YAML/tool_policy) — not reproduced here; my session had read access, so the denial was not observed.
The 'write-scope Brick + write-capable lane + aligned adapter -> admissible write' prediction at runtime — confirmable only by structure here (no live Claude run was performed, per task-source).

Whether agent_resources.py and the individual tool_policy YAML bodies fully match the plan's packet-described intersection rule — I confirmed the rule in agent_adapter.py but did not open agent_resources.py or each tool_policy file in this review.
Checker/--all status — not run (task-source excludes it); no claim.
Whether the absence of a dedicated investigation node in this chain is intended — a Link/COO composition decision, not observed truth.

agent_adapter.py:868-877 agent_request_effective_write() = bool(request.write_scope) AND READ_WRITE_TOOL_POLICY_REF in request.tool_policy_refs AND _adapter_ref_supports_observed_write(adapter_ref) — the exact 'write_scope NEED AND read-write tool-policy AND observed-write adapter' intersection the task asked to locate.
